# Evidence-Pack Manifest Schema

YAML schema for `evidence/<version>/manifest.yaml` produced by the `evidence-pack` skill.
This file is the single source of truth for the manifest shape and the per-entry field
semantics. Auditors and downstream consumers should treat this file as the contract.

> **Loading**: Loaded under `tier: standard` and `tier: deep` via the skill's `ref_docs.manifest`
> entry. Skip when invoking under `tier: light`.

## Top-Level Document Layout

```yaml
# evidence/<version>/manifest.yaml
# Generated by /evidence-pack -- do not edit by hand
_meta:
  schema: "1.0.0"           # Schema version of this document
  generated: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ"  # ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
  generator: "evidence-pack"         # Skill name that produced this artifact
  version: "<version>"      # The release version this pack documents
  git_head: "<full-sha>"    # Git commit at the time of generation
  git_tag: "<tag-or-null>"  # Exact tag matching git_head, or null
  tool_versions:
    git: "2.43.0"           # `git --version` output (verbatim)
    gh: "2.40.1"            # `gh --version` first line, or "unavailable"
  artifacts: N              # Total number of artifact entries below
  collected: N              # Subset that was successfully collected
  skipped: N                # Subset whose source was absent or tool unavailable
  failed: N                 # Subset whose collector errored

artifacts:
  - <entry>                 # See "Artifact Entry Schema" below
  - <entry>
  - ...
```

`_meta.schema` follows semantic versioning. Consumers must check `_meta.schema` before
parsing entries; mismatched majors mean the schema has changed in a backward-incompatible way.

## Artifact Entry Schema

Every entry describes one collected (or attempted) artifact and links it back to the standard
clauses whose evidence it satisfies.

```yaml
- kind: matrix                          # MANDATORY -- one of the allowed values below
  source: "docs/.index/traceability.yaml"  # MANDATORY -- repo-relative path or command line
  collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:56Z"  # MANDATORY -- ISO 8601 UTC, when collector finished
  sha256: "abc123..."                   # MANDATORY for status: collected; null otherwise
  status: collected                     # MANDATORY -- collected | skipped | failed
  reason: ""                            # OPTIONAL -- required when status is skipped or failed
  output_path: "evidence/v1.2.0/matrix/traceability.yaml"  # MANDATORY for status: collected
  related_clauses:                      # MANDATORY (may be empty) -- clause IDs this artifact bears on
    - IEC-62304-5.2.6
    - ISO-13485-7.3.6
  notes: ""                             # OPTIONAL -- free-form, single line for human reviewers
```

### Field Definitions

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `kind` | enum | Yes | One of the allowed kind values below. The kind determines which collector ran and which subdirectory the artifact lives in. |
| `source` | string | Yes | Where the artifact came from. For file kinds, the repo-relative path. For tool kinds, the verbatim command line (e.g. `gh run list --branch v1.2.0 --json ...`). The audit trail. |
| `collected_at` | string | Yes | UTC ISO 8601 timestamp at which the collector finished writing the artifact (for `status: collected`) or determined the source absent / failure (for the other statuses). |
| `sha256` | string | Conditional | 64-char lowercase hex sha256 of the collected file. Required when `status: collected`. For directory-shaped kinds the value is sha256 over the deterministic listing `find <dir> -type f \| sort \| xargs sha256sum`. Set to `null` for non-collected entries. |
| `status` | enum | Yes | `collected` (success), `skipped` (source absent or tool unavailable), `failed` (collector errored). |
| `reason` | string | Conditional | Required when `status` is `skipped` or `failed`. Short tag plus optional detail, e.g. `source_absent`, `tool_unavailable: gh not on PATH`, `failed: gh exit 1 -- network timeout`. |
| `output_path` | string | Conditional | Repo-relative path under `evidence/<version>/` where the collected artifact lives. Required when `status: collected`. Omitted otherwise. |
| `related_clauses` | list[string] | Yes (may be empty) | Clause IDs (format `<STANDARD>-<NUMBER>`, see `traceability` skill `matrix-schema.md`) this artifact bears on. Used by auditors to pivot from an artifact back to the demanding clause. |
| `notes` | string | No | Free-form, single line. Auditors read this; keep it factual. |

### Allowed `kind` Values

The kind value is a closed enum. Adding a new kind requires updating both this file and
`source-mapping.md`.

| Kind | Subdirectory | What it captures |
|------|--------------|------------------|
| `matrix` | `evidence/<version>/matrix/` | Verbatim copy of `docs/.index/traceability.yaml` produced by the sibling `traceability` skill. |
| `ci_run_log` | `evidence/<version>/ci_run_log/` | JSON output of `gh run list` plus per-run `gh run view` JSON for the most recent N runs on the release branch. |
| `pr_review` | `evidence/<version>/pr_review/` | JSON output of `gh pr list` plus per-PR `gh pr view --json reviews` for PRs merged into the release branch within the release window. |
| `signed_commits` | `evidence/<version>/signed_commits/` | Plain text from `git log --format='%H %G? %s' <prev-tag>..<version>` showing the signature status (`G`/`B`/`U`/`N`/`X`/`Y`/`R`/`E`) of every commit in the release. |
| `research_artifact` | `evidence/<version>/research_artifact/` | Mirror of files under `docs/research/` that document external citations (per the `research` skill output). |
| `risk_file` | `evidence/<version>/risk_file/` | Mirror of `risk-file/` directory contents at the time of release. |

A kind whose source is genuinely absent in a project (e.g. no `risk-file/` directory yet)
should appear in the manifest with `status: skipped` and `reason: source_absent` rather than
being omitted -- explicit absence is auditable; silent omission is not.

### Status Field

The `status` value is set by the collector in Phase 3 and then frozen.

| Status | When |
|--------|------|
| `collected` | The collector wrote at least one file under `output_path` and `sha256` is populated. |
| `skipped` | The source was absent at collection time, or a required tool was unavailable. The kind was not requested via `--include` (or the failure mode was tolerable for default collection). |
| `failed` | The collector ran but exited non-zero, raised a recoverable error, or produced corrupt output. The skill exits non-zero only when a `failed` entry corresponds to a kind explicitly requested via `--include`. |

The skill writes one of the three values; consumers must not invent new ones.

## Identifier and Format Conventions

### Clause Reference Format

`related_clauses` entries follow the same format as the `clause_refs` field in the
traceability matrix (see `global/skills/_internal/traceability/reference/matrix-schema.md`):
`<STANDARD>-<NUMBER>` where `<STANDARD>` is uppercase with hyphens preserved and `<NUMBER>`
is the dotted clause path verbatim from the standard.

Examples:

| String | Standard | Clause |
|--------|----------|--------|
| `IEC-62304-5.5.5` | IEC 62304 | 5.5.5 (Software unit verification) |
| `IEC-62304-8.2.1` | IEC 62304 | 8.2.1 (Approve change requests) |
| `ISO-13485-7.3.5` | ISO 13485 | 7.3.5 (Design and development review) |
| `ISO-13485-7.3.6` | ISO 13485 | 7.3.6 (Design and development verification) |

### sha256 Format

64-character lowercase hex, no `sha256:` prefix in the field value. The `INTEGRITY` file
sibling to `manifest.yaml` uses the prefixed form (`sha256:<hex>`); the per-entry field does
not.

### Timestamp Format

ISO 8601, UTC, second precision, `Z` suffix. Example: `2026-05-04T12:34:56Z`. No fractional
seconds, no timezone offsets other than `Z`.

## Example: Minimal Manifest

A small but complete `manifest.yaml` for a release that only ships the matrix and signed
commits (a project that has not yet wired up `gh` or a risk file):

```yaml
_meta:
  schema: "1.0.0"
  generated: "2026-05-04T12:34:56Z"
  generator: "evidence-pack"
  version: "v0.1.0"
  git_head: "0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01234567"
  git_tag: "v0.1.0"
  tool_versions:
    git: "git version 2.43.0"
    gh: "unavailable"
  artifacts: 6
  collected: 2
  skipped: 4
  failed: 0

artifacts:
  - kind: matrix
    source: "docs/.index/traceability.yaml"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:50Z"
    sha256: "9e107d9d372bb6826bd81d3542a419d6ce2d6b81234567890abcdef1234567ab"
    status: collected
    output_path: "evidence/v0.1.0/matrix/traceability.yaml"
    related_clauses:
      - IEC-62304-5.2.6
      - ISO-13485-7.3.6
    notes: ""

  - kind: ci_run_log
    source: "gh run list --branch v0.1.0 --json status,conclusion,name,databaseId,createdAt"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:51Z"
    sha256: null
    status: skipped
    reason: "tool_unavailable: gh not on PATH"
    related_clauses:
      - IEC-62304-5.5.5
      - ISO-13485-7.3.6
    notes: ""

  - kind: pr_review
    source: "gh pr list --search 'merged:<=2026-05-04 base:develop' --json number,reviews"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:51Z"
    sha256: null
    status: skipped
    reason: "tool_unavailable: gh not on PATH"
    related_clauses:
      - IEC-62304-8.2.1
      - ISO-13485-7.3.5
    notes: ""

  - kind: signed_commits
    source: "git log --format='%H %G? %s' v0.0.1..v0.1.0"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:52Z"
    sha256: "1ed3a2b87c3d2b1aef8a9e4d3c2b1a098765432109876543210fedcba9876543"
    status: collected
    output_path: "evidence/v0.1.0/signed_commits/commits.txt"
    related_clauses:
      - IEC-62304-8.2.1
      - ISO-13485-7.3.5
    notes: ""

  - kind: research_artifact
    source: "docs/research/"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:53Z"
    sha256: null
    status: skipped
    reason: "source_absent"
    related_clauses:
      - ISO-13485-7.3.3
    notes: ""

  - kind: risk_file
    source: "risk-file/"
    collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:34:53Z"
    sha256: null
    status: skipped
    reason: "source_absent"
    related_clauses:
      - IEC-62304-7.1.1
      - IEC-62304-7.2.1
    notes: ""
```

## Example: Failed Entry

```yaml
- kind: ci_run_log
  source: "gh run list --branch v1.2.0 --json status,conclusion,name,databaseId"
  collected_at: "2026-05-04T12:35:10Z"
  sha256: null
  status: failed
  reason: "failed: gh exit 4 -- HTTP 502 from api.github.com after 3 retries"
  related_clauses:
    - IEC-62304-5.5.5
    - ISO-13485-7.3.6
  notes: "retry once gh upstream is healthy and re-run with --include ci_run_log --force"
```

The `notes` field is the standard place to leave a remediation hint for the next runner.

## INTEGRITY File Format

The `INTEGRITY` sibling to `manifest.yaml` is a single-line file with a trailing newline:

```
sha256:<64-char-lowercase-hex>
```

Computed over the canonical bytes of `manifest.yaml` after sorting the `artifacts:` list by
`(kind, source)`. To verify externally:

```bash
expected=$(cut -d: -f2 evidence/<version>/INTEGRITY)
actual=$(sha256sum evidence/<version>/manifest.yaml | cut -d' ' -f1)
[ "$expected" = "$actual" ] && echo OK || echo TAMPERED
```

The `INTEGRITY` file protects only against accidental edits to the manifest itself. It is
not a cryptographic signature and confers no non-repudiation. Signed attestations are
explicitly out of scope (see issue #595 "Out of Scope").

## Schema Evolution

Schema changes are versioned via `_meta.schema`. The current major is `1`. Bump rules:

| Change | Bump |
|--------|------|
| Add a new optional field | Minor -- `1.0.0` -> `1.1.0`. Older consumers ignore the field. |
| Add a new allowed `kind` value | Minor -- existing entries unchanged; older consumers will see an unknown kind and should warn rather than fail. |
| Rename or remove a field | Major -- `1.x.x` -> `2.0.0`. Consumers must guard on the major. |
| Change the meaning of an existing field | Major. |
| Change the canonicalization rule used for `INTEGRITY` | Major (because external verifiers will produce different hashes). |

The `evidence-pack` skill writes the matching schema version into `_meta.schema` on every
generation. External tooling that consumes `manifest.yaml` should refuse to parse when the
major does not match the schema this file documents.

- Sibling matrix consumer: `../../traceability/reference/matrix-schema.md`
